output.title: Landlord Withholding Security Deposit? How to Get Your Money Back Fast
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, what landlords can legally deduct, and how to demand your money back fast.
content_outline: The Silent Treatment: Why Your Landlord Is Not Returning Your Deposit
Is My Landlord Required to Return My Deposit? State Deadlines Explained
Can a Landlord Keep Your Security Deposit? Legal Deductions vs. Wrongful Withholding
What To Do When Your Landlord Doesn't Return Your Deposit: The Demand Letter
Escalating Your Security Deposit Dispute: Court, Damages, and Next Steps
key_points: Most states require landlords to return security deposits within 14–30 days after move-out
Landlords cannot deduct for normal wear and tear; only actual damages and unpaid rent
A formal security deposit dispute letter is the fastest, most effective first step to recovery
Tenants may be entitled to statutory damages, interest, or attorneys' fees if a landlord wrongfully withholds a deposit
DepositBack generates state-specific demand letters that create legal urgency and speed up recovery
output.cta: Don't let your landlord keep what's yours. Generate your state-specific security deposit demand letter in under 2 minutes and recover your money now.
output.target_audience: Problem-aware US renters whose landlords are withholding, delaying, or refusing to return security deposits and are actively seeking a fast, legal solution to recover their money.
